message:
fuzz(corpus): expand pdu_stream_decode + termwiz_csi_parser seeds

Adds 1,369 minimized corpus entries (~28 KB total, ~20 bytes
average) across two fuzz targets:

  - fuzz/corpus/pdu_stream_decode/   —   559 new seeds
  - fuzz/corpus/termwiz_csi_parser/  —   810 new seeds

## Why

Both targets previously ran against very narrow corpora, which
bottlenecks libFuzzer's ability to find interesting mutations — it
spends most of its time re-exploring the same coverage regions.
These seeds were collected from actual fuzzing runs and minimized
(small and diverse, not duplicates of each other), so libFuzzer can
start each fresh run with a much wider coverage frontier. This is
the same pattern used in prior expansions (see commits 650a3bdf,
fbcb9f42, ad28aa99) rather than a new policy — fuzz corpora are
tracked in-repo under fuzz/corpus/ by convention, with only
fuzz/artifacts/ gitignored.

## What the seeds target

  - pdu_stream_decode: protocol-aware seeds that exercise header
    boundaries, frame-length edges, and PDU tag permutations. Many
    are ≤8 bytes so libFuzzer treats them as cheap seed expansions.
  - termwiz_csi_parser: CSI / DCS / OSC escape-sequence shapes.
    Mostly short control-char prefixes plus representative
    parameter lists — enough to cover the state-machine branches
    that only trigger on specific intermediate-byte combinations.

No production code is touched; pure corpus expansion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
